scope: Eight prompt-to-output A-roll pairs sampled from two 2025 avatar production lineages
privacyBoundary: Sanitized family labels, normalized prompt hashes, aggregate observations, and measured output properties only; no raw prompts, private project IDs, media IDs, account data, or provider credentials

method.pairing: Authenticated Flow media detail matched to the canonical private prompt record
method.promptFingerprint: SHA-256 over LF-normalized UTF-8 prompt text
method.mediaIntegrity: Original MP4 download, SHA-256, and ffprobe
method.visual: One-frame-per-second contact sheets reviewed for identity, framing, hands, facial cadence, and terminal state
method.pitch: Band-limited autocorrelation estimate; approximate and not biometric identification
method.fineLipSync: UNKNOWN; no validated fine audiovisual alignment scorer was available

families.observation: The exact word-timed precision gesture manifested, but the same gesture family became visibly repetitive when reused across a sequence.
Best relative visual result in the bounded sample: stable identity, grounded seated weight, asymmetric hand motion, and a readable expression arc.
Strong podcast framing and identity stability, but the performance reads more emphatic and presenter-like than the preferred realism lineage.
A single psychological camera move and one restrained expression beat produced a coherent clip without a dense physiology manifest.
A neutral-to-wonder-to-confidence arc manifested more naturally than repeated mechanical gesture instructions.
The compact locked-camera contract preserved the set and subject, but symmetrical hand motion remained a visible synthetic cue.
The output omitted part of the approved line; simulated coverage complexity is not a safe default for exact-dialogue A-roll.
The provider rendered a coherent clip despite duplicated conflicting JSON, but the input is rejected as non-deterministic evidence.

adopt: one explicit performance mode per shot
zero or one exact phrase-motivated hand gesture per eight seconds
separate micro-expression cues from hand gestures
cue-free restrained stillness as the default
no consecutive reuse of the same gesture type
one camera strategy selected by narrative intent
one immutable sequence voice profile with returned audio measured after every shot
a natural settle plus three-frame handoff rather than a long theatrical freeze
rejectAsDefaults: simulated multi-camera coverage for exact-dialogue clips
dense numeric physiology instructions without a causal need
treating a textual pitch or voice-profile field as proof of provider adherence
compilerDecision.claimBoundary: This bounded audit supports compiler and QC decisions. It does not establish provider-wide success rates, product limits, native voice locking, or fine lip-sync capability.
